conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility properly, I'll need to ask you some questions. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

First, are you or your partner responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you or your partner look after Alex and pay towards the costs of raising them (such as food, clothes, housing, etc.)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex. I look after them and pay for all their needs - food, clothes, housing, everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great. Now, could you tell me where you currently live? Specifically, do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A few more questions to complete the assessment:

Are you or your partner subject to immigration control, or do either of you have any restrictions on your right to reside in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we don't have any immigration restrictions or any limits on our right to live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Just to clarify - does Alex live with you full-time, or do they spend time living with anyone else (such as another parent or relative)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me full-time at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to ensure I have all the information I need:

1. Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? (This would typically mean school or college for someone their age)

2. Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
1. Yes, Alex is in school - they're 8 years old, so they're in primary school.

2. No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just read through the official eligibility rules to confirm everything, and then I'll give you a decision.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now give you my decision.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on all the information you've provided, you meet all the requirements:

✓ Alex is under 16 years old (8 years old)
✓ You are responsible for Alex and pay for their needs
✓ Alex lives with you full-time
✓ You live in the UK with no immigration restrictions
✓ Alex is in approved education (primary school)
✓ Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. 

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you would still be eligible to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) Alex is under 16 years old (8 years old), which meets the age requirement. 2) You are responsible for Alex - you live with them and pay for all their needs including food, clothes, and housing. 3) Alex lives with you full-time in the UK. 4) You live in the UK and have no immigration restrictions or limits on your right to reside. 5) Alex is in approved education (primary school). 6) Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them in the UK, they have no immigration restrictions, and no one else is claiming for the child.
